info.title: Beamable.Tools
info.description:
The Beamable.Tools package is a dotnet tool that connects to the Beamable API and allows developers
      to manage their Beamable resources via the CLI.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: A CLI for interacting with the Beamable Cloud.

commands.name: me
commands.description: Fetch the current account

commands.name: checks
commands.description: Check if your projects for known issues

commands.name: locked-files
commands.description: Check if there are any locked file inside a folder

commands.name: scan
commands.description: Scan the workspace for known issues

commands.name: config
commands.description: List the current beamable configuration

commands.name: realm
commands.description: Get current realm config values

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove realm config values

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set realm config values

commands.name: routes
commands.description: List the various service routes for your Beamable host

commands.name: secret
commands.description: Get the realm secret

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set a config value

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of a config option to set. ex: cid, pid, etc.

arguments.name: value
arguments.description: The value of the config setting

commands.name: content
commands.description: Open content folder in file explorer

commands.name: archive-manifest
commands.description: Archive an existing content manifest

arguments.name: manifest-id
arguments.description: The manifest id to archive

commands.name: list-manifests
commands.description: List all local and remote content manifest ids

commands.name: new-manifest
commands.description: Create a local empty content manifest for the current realm

arguments.name: manifest-id
arguments.description: The manifest id to create locally

commands.name: open
commands.description: Open content file in default editor

commands.name: ps
commands.description: List the running status of local services not running in docker

commands.name: publish
commands.description: Publish content and manifest

commands.name: replace-local
commands.description: Replaces the local content from a specific realm cached folder to another realm cached folder | You will lose all the content in the target realm

commands.name: resolve
commands.description: Resolve between local changes ore realm based changes

commands.name: restore
commands.description: Restore content data based on a local snapshot, remote manifest ID or manifest file

commands.name: save
commands.description: Saves a serialized content properties JSON-blob into a manifest (expects the blob to be in Beamable's Serialization Format). This command is not meant for manual usage. It is meant for engine integrations and CI/CD content enforcing use-cases.Editing of content is to be made either via engine integrations OR via a JSON text-editor

commands.name: snapshot
commands.description: Save a manifest as a contents snapshot

commands.name: snapshot-list
commands.description: Find and list all shared (.beamable/content-snapshots/[PID]) and local (.beamable/temp/content-snapshots/[PID]) snapshots

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show current status of the content

commands.name: sync
commands.description: Synchronizes the local content matching the filters to the latest content stored in the realm

commands.name: tag
commands.description: Commands for adding/set/removing tags

commands.name: add
commands.description: Adds tag to content

arguments.name: tag
arguments.description: List of tags for us to affect

commands.name: rm
commands.description: Removes tag from content

arguments.name: tag
arguments.description: List of tags for us to affect

commands.name: set
commands.description: Set tag to content

arguments.name: tag
arguments.description: List of tags for us to affect

commands.name: deployment
commands.description: Commands for interacting with microservice and microstorage deployments

commands.name: get
commands.description: Get a specific deployment

commands.name: list
commands.description: List the deployments

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Plan a deployment for later release

commands.name: release
commands.description: Perform a release, this will modify remote running services

commands.name: status
commands.description: Show the current deployment status

commands.name: registry
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Find all docker image tags that exist for the given beamoId

arguments.name: service-id
arguments.description: The beamo id for the service to get images for

commands.name: developer-user-manager
commands.description: The command that manages the developer users

commands.description: Clean up the captured users files based on a rolling buffer, the criteria is the oldest to the newer

commands.description: Copy the inventory and stats state from a user to another

commands.name: create-user
commands.description: Create a single user that can be from a template or just create new empty user

commands.name: create-user-batch
commands.description: Create multiple users from multiples templates doing the request in parallel

commands.name: ps
commands.description: The ps commands manage the files for the developer users folders, it can keep watching changes in the file system and emit events to the engine

commands.name: remove-user
commands.description: Remove a user or a list from the local files, it will not remove it from the portal

commands.name: save-user
commands.description: Create a new developer user in the local files

commands.name: update-info
commands.description: Update the info of the developer user (Alias, Description etc)

arguments.description: The service to disable federation

commands.description: Enable an active federation without stopping a service

arguments.description: The service to disable federation

commands.description: Get the local settings for the Beamable.Common.IFederatedGameServer`1[T]  local routing key

commands.name: set
commands.description: Sets the local settings for any particular federation

commands.name: IFederatedGameServer
commands.description: Get the local settings for the Beamable.Common.IFederatedGameServer`1[T]  local routing key

arguments.description: the folder that will be initialized as a beamable project.  [default: .]

commands.name: listen
commands.description: Listen to events

commands.description: Listen for player notifications

commands.name: server
commands.description: Listen to server events

commands.name: login
commands.description: Save credentials

commands.name: logout
commands.description: Removes any saved credentials

commands.name: oapi
commands.description: Commands that integrate the Beamable API and open API

commands.name: player
commands.description: Get information about a player

commands.description: Open portal

commands.name: add-paths
commands.description: Add extra paths and ignore paths for services

commands.description: Add a unity project to this beamable cli project

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Relative path to the Unity project

commands.name: add-unreal-project
commands.description: Add a unreal project to this beamable cli project

arguments.name: path
arguments.description: Relative path to the Unreal project

commands.name: build
commands.description: Build and monitor for compile errors

commands.name: dependencies
commands.description: List project dependencies

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the service

commands.name: deps
commands.description: Allow access to dependencies related commands

commands.name: add
commands.description: Add a given storage as a dependency of a microservice

arguments.name: microservice
arguments.description: The microservice name that the dependency will be added to

arguments.name: dependency
arguments.description: The storage that will be a dependency of the given microservice

commands.name: list
commands.description: Lists all dependencies of given service, if none then lists all dependencies of all existing services

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove the dependency between the given microservice and storage

arguments.name: microservice
arguments.description: The microservice name that the dependency will be removed from

arguments.name: dependency
arguments.description: The dependency that will be removed from that service

commands.name: disable
commands.description: Disables a project. This modifies the BeamEnabled setting in the given project files

commands.name: enable
commands.description: Enables a project. This modifies the BeamEnabled setting in the given project files

commands.description: Commands that allows generating various clients for Beamable services

commands.name: web-client
commands.description: Generate TypeScript/JavaScript Client Code for Microservices

commands.name: generate-client-oapi
commands.description: Generate Client Code from OAPI specifications

commands.name: generate-ignore-file
commands.description: Generate an ignore file in .beamable folder for given VCS

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: Which VCS to generate the ignore file for

commands.name: generate-properties
commands.description: Generates a Directory.Build.props file with the beam path and solution dir

arguments.name: output
arguments.description: Where the file will be created

arguments.name: beam-path
arguments.description: Beam path to be used. Use BEAM_SOLUTION_DIR to template in $(SolutionDir)

arguments.name: solution-dir
arguments.description:
The solution path to be used. 
The following values have special meaning and are not treated as paths... 
- "DIR.PROPS" = $([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName(`$(DirectoryBuildPropsPath)`))

commands.name: rm
commands.description: Remove a group from a project

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: Name of the service

arguments.name: groups
arguments.description: Beamable groups for this service

commands.description: Get a list of microservices

arguments.name: service

arguments.name: name

commands.name: oapi
commands.description: Generate the Open API specification for the project

commands.description: List the running status of local services not running in docker

commands.description: Regenerate the solution csproj, Dockerfile and Program.cs files

arguments.name: name

arguments.description: Where the temp project will be created [default: ]

commands.description: Removes the local source code for a service or storage

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: The .dll filepath for the built code

commands.description: Create a snapshot of a local Storage Object

arguments.name: beamoId
arguments.description: The beamoId for the storage object

commands.name: update-unity-beam-package
commands.description: Updates or adds Beamable packages to Unity project

arguments.name: path

commands.name: generate-client
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Obsolete command, please use generate-client-oapi that used the OpenAPI specifications to generate the C# client code. The generate-client command will Generate a C# client file based on a built C# microservice dll directory using refactor

arguments.name: source
arguments.description: The .dll filepath for the built microservice

commands.name: generate-env
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Get the connection strings required to start a Microservice. This is used when running Standalone Microservices locally without Docker

arguments.name: service
arguments.description: Which service to generate the .env file for

arguments.name: output
arguments.description: Where to output the .env file

commands.name: read-settings

commands.description: [INTERNAL] get remote logs for a service

arguments.description: The beamo id for the service to get logs for

commands.name: remote-manifest
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Returns the remote manifest in json format

commands.name: write-setting
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Write a group of project settings

arguments.name: beamoId
arguments.description: The BeamoId to write the settings for

commands.name: telemetry
commands.description: Allows access to Open Telemetry related commands

commands.name: collector
commands.description: Allows access to Open Telemetry collector related commands

commands.name: get
commands.description: Downloads the otel collector

commands.name: ps
commands.description: Starts a stream of messages containing the status of the collector

commands.name: start
commands.description: Starts the collector process

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Stops the collector running process

commands.name: config
commands.description: Retrieves Beam Open Telemetry configuration

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Fetch logs from Clickhouse

arguments.name: filter
arguments.description: Value to be matched with the log message body [default: ]

commands.name: prune
commands.description: Clears telemetry data that was exported to files by normal usage of the CLI

commands.name: push
commands.description: Pushes local telemetry data saved through the BeamableExporter to a running collector. This uses the Open Telemetry OTLP exporter to push telemetry from files to a running collector using Http protocol

commands.name: set-config
commands.description: Sets the Beam CLI otel configuration

arguments.name: cli-log-level
arguments.description: The minimum Open Telemetry LogLevel to be sent to Clickhouse, this needs to be a valid LogLevel converted to string value

arguments.name: cli-telemetry-max-size
arguments.description: The maximum size in bytes for saved Otel data

arguments.name: cli-allow-telemetry
arguments.description: Define if the telemetry is allowed to push informations to beamable server

commands.name: get-creds
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Get credentials to read telemetry data from Beamable

commands.name: report
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Sends custom telemetry data to CLI to be saved in file. Runs `beam otel push` to send this data to ClickHouse

commands.name: temp
commands.description: Commands for the .beamable temp folder

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Commands to clear files in the temp folder

commands.name: logs
commands.description: Clear temp log files

commands.name: version
commands.description: Commands for managing the CLI version

commands.name: install
commands.description: Install a different version of the CLI

arguments.name: version
arguments.description: The version of the CLI to install [default: latest]

commands.name: list
commands.description: Show the most recent available versions

commands.name: construct
commands.description: [INTERNAL] constructs a beamable version string with the given configuration

arguments.name: major
arguments.description: The major semantic version number

arguments.name: minor
arguments.description: The minor semantic version number

arguments.name: patch
arguments.description: The patch semantic version number

commands.name: docs
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Generate CLI documentation

commands.name: generate-interface
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Generates C# code for interfacing with the CLI from Unity

commands.description: [INTERNAL] Allows access to a local Grafana instance

commands.description: Opens a local Grafana installation to inspect telemetry data

commands.name: ps
commands.description: Check if a local Grafana is running for the project

commands.name: stop
commands.description: Stop the locally running Grafana

commands.name: mkdocs
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Generate the mkdoc command suite documentation

commands.name: profile
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Commands for inspecting profiling reports

commands.name: check-counters
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Read the results of a dotnet-counters json file and determine if there are errors

arguments.name: counters-file-path
arguments.description: The path to the dotnet-counters output json file

commands.name: check-nbomber
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Read the results of a n-bomber .csv file and determine if there are errors

arguments.name: nbomber-file-path
arguments.description: The path to the nbomber output csv file

commands.name: run-nbomber
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Runs an n-bomber stress test for a given microservice method

arguments.name: service
arguments.description: The name of the microservice to stress test

arguments.name: method
arguments.description: The method name in the service to stress test

commands.name: server
commands.description: [INTERNAL] The CLI can be run as a server

commands.name: clear
commands.description: Kill a running server instance

commands.name: ps
commands.description: List out available CLI servers

commands.name: req
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Request a CLI invocation from a running CLI server

commands.name: serve
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Create a local server for the cli

arguments.name: owner
arguments.description: The owner of the server is used to identify the server later with the /info endpoint [default: cli]

commands.name: services
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Commands that allow interacting with microservices in Beamable project

commands.name: build
commands.description: [REMOVED] Build a set of services into docker images

commands.description: Commands for managing docker

commands.description: Start the docker daemon

commands.description: Check if docker is running and available to the CLI

commands.name: get-connection-string
commands.description: Gets the Microstorage connection string

arguments.name: storage-name
arguments.description: The name of the Microstorage

commands.name: manifests
commands.description: [REMOVED] Outputs manifests json to console

commands.name: promote
commands.description: Promotes the manifest from the given 'sourcePid' to your current realm

commands.name: ps
commands.description: [REMOVED] Lists the current local or remote service manifest and status (as summary table or json)

commands.name: registry
commands.description: Gets the docker registry URL that we upload docker images into when deploying services remotely for this realm

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Clean up docker related resources

commands.name: container
commands.description: Delete any containers associated with the given Beamable services

commands.name: image
commands.description: [REMOVED] Delete any images associated with the given Beamable services

commands.name: run
commands.description: [REMOVED] Run services locally in Docker. Will fail if no docker instance is running in the local machine

commands.name: service-logs
commands.description: [REMOVED] Gets the URL that we can use to see logs our service is emitting

commands.name: service-metrics
commands.description: [REMOVED] Gets the URL that we can use to see the metrics for our services

commands.name: stop
commands.description: [REMOVED] Stops all your locally running containers for the selected Beamo Services

commands.name: templates
commands.description: [REMOVED] Gets all the template types available in this realm

commands.name: upload-api
commands.description: Gets the URL that we upload docker images into when deploying services remotely for this realm

commands.name: deploy
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Deploys services remotely to the current realm

commands.name: generate-manifest
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Generate a local manifest by scraping local files

commands.name: update-dockerfile
commands.description: [INTERNAL] [REMOVED] Updates the Dockerfile for the specified service

arguments.name: ServiceName
arguments.description: The name of the microservice to udpate the Dockerfile

commands.name: token
commands.description: [INTERNAL] explore Beamable tokens

commands.name: from-refresh
commands.description: Get an access token from a refresh token

commands.name: inspect
commands.description: Get token information

commands.name: list
commands.description: List tokens for a player or account

commands.name: new-guest
commands.description: Create a new guest token

commands.name: unity
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Commands that are specific to Unity game integrations

commands.name: manifest
commands.description: Read local file state and show your local manifest information

commands.name: restore
commands.description: Restore a dotnet project

commands.name: copy-dotnet-src
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Copy the src from a dotnet csproj project into a Unity project

arguments.name: csprojPath
arguments.description: path to csproj project

arguments.name: unityPath
arguments.description: relative path to Unity destination for src files

commands.name: download-all-nuget-packages
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Download all known beamable nuget deps for the Beamable SDK

arguments.name: unityProjectPath
arguments.description: the path to the Unity project

commands.name: download-nuget-package
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Download a beamable nuget package dep into Unity

arguments.name: packageId
arguments.description: the nuget id of the package dep

arguments.name: packageVersion
arguments.description: the version of the package

arguments.name: src
arguments.description: the file path inside the package to copy

arguments.name: dst
arguments.description: the target location to place the copied files

commands.name: get-version-info
commands.description: [INTERNAL] get information about a beamable unity sdk project's version dependencies

arguments.name: unityPath
arguments.description: the path to the root of the unity project

commands.name: release-shared-code
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Copy the various shared code projects into the Beamable Unity SDK

arguments.name: csprojPath
arguments.description: path to csproj project

arguments.name: unityPath
arguments.description: relative path to Unity destination for src files

arguments.name: packageId
arguments.description: the name of the package to copy into

arguments.name: packageRelativePath
arguments.description: relative path to Unity destination for src files

arguments.name: relativeSrc
arguments.description: relative path to src files

commands.name: update-dlls
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Update all DLLs references of the service

arguments.name: service
arguments.description: The name of the service to update the dlls

commands.name: update-references
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Updates all Unity Assembly Definition references of the specified service

arguments.name: service
arguments.description: The name of the service to update the references

commands.name: unreal
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Commands that are specific to Unreal game integrations

commands.name: select-sample
commands.description: Run this ONLY when inside the root of the UnrealSDK repo to configure it as a particular sample

arguments.name: sample-name
arguments.description: The name of the sample with or without "BEAMPROJ_"

commands.name: init
commands.description: [INTERNAL] Ran by `beam_init_game_maker.sh` in order to do a bunch of set up in an Unreal project automatically

arguments.name: unrealSdkRepoPath
arguments.description: Path to your clone of Beamable's UnrealSDK repo in your machine

arguments.name: uprojectFilePath
arguments.description: Path to the .uproject file of your project

arguments.name: installOss
arguments.description: Whether or not we are installing the OnlineSubsystemBeamable
